review_title: Lots of features as open-source & enterprise group chat software
description: Rocket.Chat has been very useful and relatively low-maintenance. Especially since we deployed it in MongoDB Atlas (another commercial product as DBaaS), so our MongoDB cluster is also low-maintenance. There are many features that can be enabled and configured, it's quite overwhelming at first. (to be honest, it still is now) But it makes me feel good too because if there's a use case, I feel that maybe 60-70% of the time, Rocket.Chat already provides that as an option.
author: Hendy I.
pros: The core software is open-source, so the cost is low to get started. You have the option, but not forced to, to purchase business/enterprise support and upgrade for additional features, and Rocket.Chat company also provides hosting services. In case you want to modify the code (likely you won't need it), but you can, so it's a big plus for feeling in control. Rocket.Chat supports OpenID Connect for SSO authentication out-of-the-box, which we integrate with FusionAuth and it works as expected. So really happy about this. We also did submit some patches and enhancements to Rocket.Chat GitHub, and while response time varies, we were welcomed, some of our contributions were accepted, some were not accepted but with clear explanation. So we're very happy that we can collaborate directly with Rocket.Chat developers and hopefully having good relationship in the long term.
cons: I still see stability issues, sometimes chats not refreshing, and random minor issues. It's quite hard to debug/diagnose the issue, and on GitHub some issues have been waiting for weeks or months. I understand this is complex code without various platforms connected together, and to be fair Rocket.Chat tried to provide several debugging options inside it, so they do put effort in this. Configuring to use own Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) push notification is tricky, and requires deploying customized Android and iOS app... but this is to be expected and I'm not sure how it can be made easier. It still feels that they're pushing for new features more than to fix bugs. Which can be both a good thing or bad thing, depending on which side you're getting.

review_title: This OpenSource tool made our whole business possible
description: We started our self-managed hosted community version of Rocket.Chat a week before starting our company. We were able to quickly facilitate collaboration and communication in our firm. We have implemented a custom made chatbot linked to our MySQL database for work distribution. This tool have been and still is the best we have used in the last two years. It is our "remote office" base. Amazing software, very customizable, unlimited possibilities.
author: Julien D.
pros: The possibility of having a self-managed version of it, running on our own servers, for free. This ensured our data stayed ours. We also implemented a chatbot connected to our MySQL and automatized the work distribution.
cons: There are over 1200 permission settings. Around 50% of the software is "okay" documented, the rest indicates it is "under development". Very complex to know exactly what impact will have each setting.

review_title: Rocket Chat - Good But not Great
description: Rocket Chat is a cost-effective communication solution for small businesses. However, when compared to its competitors (particularly Slack), it's not a product I'd suggest to someone trying to get the most out of it. The development team must work harder to stabilize the program and make it more user-friendly.
author: Felix K.
pros: 1. I like the notification system. It's highly unlikely that you will miss a crucial notification. When the status is set to busy, the notifications will stay in the background 2. It is definitely cheaper compared to its competitors 3. It's good for team collaboration and communication with the ability to create changes 4. The emojis spice up the communication 5. It also offers SSO functionality where users can log in with their GSUITE credentials
cons: 1. The major con has to be the User Interface. It could definitely use a lot of improvements to make it smooth 2. When starting it for the first time, the servers actually take forever to load 3. It hangs quite a lot even with a very strong internet connection and high spec laptop 4. The file sharing option could be made smoother. 5. The video call option is still missing 6. It's very easy to miss notifications while using the mobile app

review_title: My 6 months with Rocket Chat
description: Very satisfied . I have used Slack, Teamwork Chat before, but Rocket Chat is very economical to me ( free- minus the VPS cost). ANd it has everything we need.
author: Aditya A.
pros: 1. Installation is a breeze. I was able to install Rocket Chat, get SSL configured, and had a running server set up in an hour without having to wave through endless linux commands. 2. Inbuilt support, for Video call using Jitsi, and inbuilt notifications. 3. Slack like user interface , which my team can instantly connect to.
cons: 1. The Quote a message, can be improved, it is visually not very clear. 2. The Video call doesn't ring inside the application, only gives a button to join which makes people skip it some times. 3. On Windows, if the app is running in background, you need to go to the notification panel to bring it to front, if you start from the Start Menu, it doesn't come to front. Overall, the shortcomings are very less and trivial. The software is very stable and a breeze to use.

review_title: An open source scalable chat platform
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Rocket Chat is a completely open source and scalable platform. I love all the options that are offered from: message history, public & private channels, video, admin options, and more. The options are unlimited and I don't think I would ever use them all; but I'm glad they are there.
cons: Support & Sales are diffcult to contact. I like talking to an actually person over the phone when making larger scale changes to my organization.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Good & free corporate chat alternative
description: Rocket.Chat is an overall-nice & free alternative to other enterprise messaging tools. A few words about setting it up: - The server runs on-premises, so all chat data currently resides in our own infrastructure - this was also one of the reasons we chose this. - Secondly it supports LDAP, so this played well with our Active Directory accounts. - Voice calling requires additional setup, which we decided to skip. - Mimetypes for supported file attachments need to be configured by hand, one-by-one And about usability: first of all, it does lack some nice-to-haves here and there: - It cannot be integrated with SharePoint, so there are no status updates when going in a meeting. - Since the desktop client is an Electron app, notifications are a bit lacking; these aren't persistent and besides showing a small blip in the system tray icon, you can't really tell whether there are any new messages. - Also due to the client being an Electron app, it cannot support systemwide shortcuts - It lacks the "threading discussion" that you see in other chat apps. However, all basic features are in place. Pasting images and links gives you a nice preview and text formatting is simple and works as expected. Also, it has a nice Off-The-Record mode where messages are not persisted. And finally, features are still being developed so new features are added constantly.
author: Gabriel C.
pros: - Supports LDAP authentication - On-premises server - Platform availability (both web, mobile and Desktop) - Good & clean usability
cons: - Some things are still not working in the mobile native app (e.g.: voting on polls) - No support for threaded chat - No straightforward integration with SharePoint - Electron client app only, so notifications are a bit lacking
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: We switched back to Slack
description: From a hosting perspective it has been running good, but our users do not like the experience comparing it to Slack.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Rocket.chat is a great alternative to Slack with the possibility to run it without license costs on-premise. Documentation is good and a lot of Slack integrations also works with rocket.chat.
cons: Push notifications has been bugging and recently limits has been introduced for the push gateway. Our users does not like it because it is not Slack.

review_title: Simply great messaging software for discussion and chat
description: My journey so far with Rocket.Chat has been a satisfying one, it’s been a great tool for instant chat, channels and document sharing.
author: Taylor A.
pros: It’s bulk instant messaging is the most effective feature than in any other chat application which made our group chat extremely efficient.
cons: I don’t see any feature mistakes but if we want to take an action such as deleting chats it can take a bit some time.

review_title: Rocket.Chat is a Brilliant Tool for A/V/W Conferencing
author: Kelvin K.
pros: Rocket.Chat is an open-source program which offers a secure and personalized messaging, audio and video calling capabilities in one place.
cons: I do not encounter challenges when utilizing Rocket.Chat on my end.

review_title: userfriendly
description: overall I am happy with this app, nice and userfriendly
author: shauna G.
pros: I liked being able to have group conversations.
cons: trying to keep up with the conversations while typing my response.

review_title: One of the least annoying chats
description: overall is one of the least annoying and got my question answered without jumping through hoops
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: quick easy to the point. Not alot excess questions
cons: All chats are annoying we want to talk to a person
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Rocket Chat Makes virtual chat a Cinch
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like that you can post a message in real time along with pinging a specific person or even using an emoji to decorate the message. I also like that you favorite a room so it remains on your conversations list, you can update your profile and settings fairly easy and that you can edit or delete a message you posted if needed.
cons: I do not like when the software lags at times , where it takes almost a minute for it to catch up to the current messages. I also do not like that sometimes the pinned messages feature works and sometimes it doesnt. You can pin a message to it but you wont be able to see it, im not sure if theres a character count or not with that. I also do not like when you do a direct message or private message with an individual theres no way to delete the message when its not needed anymore, you can only hide the message.

review_title: Simple UI app for effective team communications
description: Good app for communications with useful features such as selective tagging and color coding of names and mentions. Replies to messages can be made through emojis leading to less clutter and spam massages. Replies can also be made in a thread like manner to any messages leading to very orderly display of messages.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Selective tagging of teammates inside a particular chat and the option to get notifications only from direct tagged mentions. Replies to messages in thread like manner and emoji replies help in keeping the UI orderly and have less clutter.
cons: Significant delays in updating of messages leading to improper communications during critical project times. Takes several attempts of refreshing to get the latest messages and mentions.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: default collaboration tool, plug and play
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Easy to use, good quality, intuitive for users with little technology skills
cons: Can be expanded to more user cases with utilising more of the features. consequently make it harder for users with less computer skills to catch up with other advanced users.

review_title: Another collaborative tool you can use for your team
description: Overall, I have a good experience using Rocket.Chat. As a user and not an admin though, I think it's quite disturbing not to know where's the limit of my privacy.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's a collaborative tool. It has got an app that you can install into all your collaborators system. As other ones, you can use to direct chat and talk to your team - and them to each other. You can put constraints and boundaries into the tool itself.
cons: As a collaborator using it and not an admin, I really don't know how it works in terms of privacy. I am sometimes wondering if admins can see all we talk about at work etc.

review_title: RC review
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: its very useful to chat with the colleagues and also to share certain type of files
cons: it doesn't have a calling option to do a video conferencing

review_title: Rocket Chat review
description: I used other professional chat applications also, it is similar to others.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's really good to use and good to create a public and private channel to do conversation in group. I also love the future of rocket chat API, so you can do a message programmatically.
cons: sometimes it's really slow to use and it crashed while opening its application. one more thing is when we search into chat, by click on that message we can not directly go to that part of the chat.

review_title: Keep up the good work
description: The main bussines problem is described in section about alternative products. Overall the experience of using Rocket Chat is good. The administration panel is way to complex (however the features are great). What bothers me currently is: * macOS automatic app updates regularly don't work and I've to install the app manualy when new version is released. * when issue arrises I, as a paying customer, find it challenging to contact customer support. I got to your main page, fill out many fields and get responsive in few days.
author: Rihards Š.
pros: - Wide functionallity, configuration, integration options. - Mobile apps are with good quality when I'm on the go.
cons: - Automatic upgrades regularly don't work (described bellow in overall experience). - Admin panel is a bit complex to configure.

review_title: Blast off with Rocket.Chat ;)
description: We got Rocket.Chat because we needed the ability to control and encrypt our own chat data for PCI compliance. We had Slack before and loved it, but never paid for it, and needed to be able to secure our own chat data, so we changed to Rocket.Chat.
author: Mark T.
pros: It's a great business chat software and makes it very simple to create channels and set chat preferences.
cons: It's not very visually appealing compared to chat software like Slack.

review_title: Rocket.Chat is good, but needs some work
author: Bruno D.
pros: Rocket.Chat is an awesome solution for enterprise chat. As a remote employee, its my main tool to connect with my peers. It enables me in a easy way to contact and share information and files. Also, since the company hosts it, I find it secure to share sensitive data
cons: The big problem with Rocket.Chat is usability. The mac application has a poor implementation , that resembles old chat applications like MSN Messenger. Its an outdated design that needs innovation ASAP

review_title: Great product, user friendly, feature rich, ldap connectivity and doesn't require lots of ram, hd.
description: I like it, will need to see how my users experiences are. This was easy to deploy but customizing it was a learning curve but with a little research, figured out how to do it. Half my org uses MS TEAMS and comparting it to Rocket.Chat has me pretty amazed how feature rich rocket chat really is. Make sure if you want video chat capability to enable JITSI in the server admin console. One but I found was embedded video chat window doesn't display users webcam but if I detach that, webcam is displayed.
author: Charles H.
pros: Relatively easy to install considering its a snap included in the Ubuntu 20 server bundle. Free to install, easy to use. Great for document collaboration, video calling and basic chat and integrates well with Microsoft AD.
cons: Found several bugs in newest version. The software overall is stable but I personally have seen half a dozen bugs while testing this including LDAP integration and listing users names; some of which are included but not displayed in the client. I wish this had a capability to see all activated users and the presence status of each user in the admin console.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: One of the best free chats
description: It works nicely (desktop and mobile), very intuitive.
author: Jose G.
pros: We use rocket chat in our company. It was the most convenient switch after (sad) years using Skype. Rocket is a rich tool for collaboration: group chats, file sharing, privileges, chat customization, etc. All at a cost of zero! It's open source meaning there is a lot of eyes at this product code and quality. Updates are available often too.
cons: It is not possible to quote a message originated in a different chat room, so if you have an important message to share for others, most likely you end up copying it and then pasting.

review_title: Rocket Chat
author: Yamini P.
pros: Good product for communication for smaller organisation. We can create private channels and it helps us to improve overall productivity by discussing projects as well sharing new ideas. Best things that I like the most are audio and video conferencing and screen sharing.
cons: Only load image files as well don't have file versioning. It is also lacking in calendar and contact sharing.

review_title: Great corporate chat
author: Maria Z.
pros: Rocket Chat is easy to use, our team uses it daily! Intuitive and high-quality chat with all functions needed (e.g. private chats, hidden chats, ability to edit or delete messages). Our team's been using it since 2018 and we're extremely happy to communicate via Rocket Chat! Thanks for doing this, guys!
cons: I'd change nothing, it'sa cool communication tool for businesses

review_title: Rocket rocks!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Ease to use, compatible with another apps and union of 3 others soluctions
cons: Integrate video conference without third part and better report on livechat
